heading: U.S. nuclear fuel security initiative
label: 16282 U.S. nuclear fuel security initiative

children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Nuclear Fuel Security Act of 2023”.

children.label: A
children.text: support increased domestic production of low-enriched uranium; and

children.label: B
children.text: accelerate efforts to establish a domestic high-assay, low-enriched uranium enrichment capability; and

children.label: A
children.text: all viable options to make high-assay, low-enriched uranium produced from inventories owned by the Department available in a manner that is sufficient to maximize the potential for the Department to meet the needs and schedules of advanced nuclear reactor developers, without impacting existing Department missions, until such time that commercial enrichment and deconversion capability for high-assay, low-enriched uranium exists at a scale sufficient to meet future needs; and

children.label: B
children.text: all viable options for partnering with countries that are allies or partners of the United States to meet those needs and schedules until that time.
to support domestic production of low-enriched uranium;

children.label: A
children.text: advanced nuclear reactor developers; and

children.label: B
children.text: the consortium;

to ensure the availability of domestically produced, converted, enriched, deconverted, and reduced uranium in a quantity determined by the Secretary, in consultation with U.S. nuclear energy companies, to be sufficient to address a reasonably anticipated supply disruption;

to address gaps and deficiencies in the domestic production, conversion, enrichment, deconversion, and reduction of uranium by partnering with countries that are allies or partners of the United States if domestic options are not practicable;

to ensure that, in the event of a supply disruption in the nuclear fuel market, a reserve of nuclear fuels is available to serve as a backup supply to support the nuclear nonproliferation and civil nuclear energy objectives of the Department, including collaborative research and development activities with other Federal agencies;
to support enrichment, deconversion, and reduction technology deployed in the United States; and

children.label: A
children.text: all viable options to make high-assay, low-enriched uranium produced from inventories owned by the Department available in a manner that is sufficient to maximize the potential for the Department to meet the needs and schedules of advanced nuclear reactor developers; and

children.label: B
children.text: all viable options for partnering with countries that are allies or partners of the United States to meet those needs and schedules.

children.heading: Advanced nuclear reactor
children.label: 1 Advanced nuclear reactor
children.text: The term “advanced nuclear reactor” has the meaning given the term in .section 16271(b) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the government of a country that is an ally or partner of the United States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an associated individual; or

children.label: B
children.text: is organized under the laws of, or otherwise subject to the jurisdiction of, a country that is an ally or partner of the United States, including a corporation that is incorporated in such a country.

children.heading: Associated individual
children.label: 3 Associated individual
children.text: The term “associated individual” means an alien who is a national of a country that is an ally or partner of the United States.

children.heading: Consortium
children.label: 4 Consortium
children.text: The term “consortium” means the consortium established under .section 16281(a)(2)(F) of this title

children.heading: Department
children.label: 5 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Energy.

children.heading: High-assay, low-enriched uranium; HALEU
children.label: 6 High-assay, low-enriched uranium; HALEU
children.text: The term “high-assay, low-enriched uranium” or “HALEU” means high-assay low-enriched uranium (as defined in ).section 16281(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: low-enriched uranium (as defined in section 2297h) of this title; and

children.label: B
children.text: low-enriched uranium (as defined in ).section 2297h–10a(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the Nuclear Fuel Security Program established under subsection (e)(1);

children.label: B
children.text: the American Assured Fuel Supply Program of the Department; and

children.label: C
children.text: the HALEU for Advanced Nuclear Reactor Demonstration Projects Program established under subsection (e)(3).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 9 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy.

children.label: A
children.text: is organized under the laws of, or otherwise subject to the jurisdiction of, the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: is involved in the nuclear energy industry.

children.heading: Establishment and expansion of programs
children.label: e Establishment and expansion of programs

children.text: establish a program, to be known as the “Nuclear Fuel Security Program”, to increase the quantity of HALEU and, if determined to be necessary after completion of a market evaluation, LEU produced by U.S. nuclear energy companies;

expand the American Assured Fuel Supply Program of the Department to ensure the availability of domestically produced, converted, enriched, deconverted, and reduced uranium in the event of a supply disruption; and

children.label: A
children.text: to maximize the potential for the Department to meet the needs and schedules of advanced nuclear reactor developers until such time that commercial enrichment and deconversion capability for HALEU exists in the United States at a scale sufficient to meet future needs; and

children.label: B
children.text: where practicable, to partner with countries that are allies or partners of the United States to meet those needs and schedules until that time.

children.label: i
children.text: if determined to be necessary or appropriate based on the completion of a market evaluation, not later than 90 days after , take actions, including cost-shared financial agreements, milestone-based payments, or other mechanisms, to support commercial availability of LEU and to promote diversity of supply in domestic uranium mining, conversion, enrichment, and deconversion capacity and technologies, including new capacity, among U.S. nuclear energy companies;December 22, 2023

children.label: ii
children.text: not later than 180 days after , enter into 2 or more contracts with members of the consortium to begin acquiring not less than 20 metric tons per year of HALEU by (or the earliest operationally feasible date thereafter), from U.S. nuclear energy companies;December 22, 2023December 31, 2027

children.label: I
children.text: the United States; or

children.label: II
children.text: if domestic options are not practicable, a country that is an ally or partner of the United States; and

children.label: iv
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, ensure that the use of domestic uranium utilized as a result of that program does not negatively affect the economic operation of nuclear reactors in the United States; and

children.label: i
children.text: may not make commitments under this subsection (including cooperative agreements (used in accordance with ), purchase agreements, guarantees, leases, service contracts, or any other type of commitment) for the purchase or other acquisition of HALEU or LEU unless funds are specifically provided for those purposes in advance in appropriations Acts enacted after ; andsection 6305 of title 31March 9, 2024

children.label: I
children.text: if the full extent of the anticipated costs stemming from the commitment is recorded as an obligation at the time that the commitment is made; and

children.label: II
children.text: to the extent of that up-front obligation recorded in full at that time.

children.label: i
children.text: partnerships between 2 or more commercial enrichers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: utilization of up to 10-percent enriched uranium as feedstock in demonstration-scale or commercial HALEU enrichment facilities;

children.label: B
children.text: options to partner with countries that are allies or partners of the United States to provide LEU and HALEU for commercial purposes;

children.label: i
children.text: enrichment levels;

children.label: ii
children.text: output levels to meet demand; and

children.label: iii
children.text: fuel forms, including uranium metal and oxide; and

children.label: i
children.text: to replenish, as necessary, Department stockpiles of uranium that were intended to be downblended for other purposes, but were instead used in carrying out activities under the HALEU for Advanced Nuclear Reactor Demonstration Projects Program;

children.label: ii
children.text: to continue supplying HALEU to meet the needs of the recipients of an award made pursuant to the funding opportunity announcement of the Department numbered DE–FOA–0002271 for Pathway 1, Advanced Reactor Demonstrations; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to make HALEU available to other advanced nuclear reactor developers and other end-users.

children.heading: Avoidance of market disruptions
children.label: 3 Avoidance of market disruptions
children.text: In carrying out the Nuclear Fuel Security Program, the Secretary, to the extent practicable and consistent with the purposes of that program, shall not disrupt or replace market mechanisms by competing with U.S. nuclear energy companies.

children.heading: Expansion of the American Assured Fuel Supply Program
children.label: g Expansion of the American Assured Fuel Supply Program

children.text: expand the American Assured Fuel Supply Program of the Department by merging the operations of the Uranium Reserve Program of the Department with the American Assured Fuel Supply Program; and

children.label: A
children.text: maintain, replenish, diversify, or increase the quantity of uranium made available by that program in a manner determined by the Secretary to be consistent with the purposes of that program and the objectives described in subsection (c);

children.label: i
children.text: the United States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if domestic options are not practicable, a country that is an ally or partner of the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: make uranium available from the American Assured Fuel Supply, subject to terms and conditions determined by the Secretary to be reasonable and appropriate;

children.label: D
children.text: refill and expand the supply of uranium in the American Assured Fuel Supply, including by maintaining a limited reserve of uranium to address a potential event in which a domestic or foreign recipient of uranium experiences a supply disruption for which uranium cannot be obtained through normal market mechanisms or under normal market conditions; and

children.label: E
children.text: take other actions that the Secretary determines to be necessary or appropriate to address the purposes of that program and the objectives described in subsection (c).

children.heading: HALEU for Advanced Nuclear Reactor Demonstration Projects Program
children.label: h HALEU for Advanced Nuclear Reactor Demonstration Projects Program

children.heading: Activities
children.label: 1 Activities
children.text: On enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall immediately accelerate and, as necessary, initiate activities to make available from inventories or stockpiles owned by the Department and made available to the consortium, HALEU for use in advanced nuclear reactors that cannot operate on uranium with lower enrichment levels or on alternate fuels, with priority given to the awards made pursuant to the funding opportunity announcement of the Department numbered DE–FOA–0002271 for Pathway 1, Advanced Reactor Demonstrations, with additional HALEU to be made available to other advanced nuclear reactor developers, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: by , not less than 3 metric tons of HALEU;September 30, 2024

children.label: B
children.text: by , not less than an additional 8 metric tons of HALEU; andDecember 31, 2025

children.label: C
children.text: by , not less than an additional 10 metric tons of HALEU.June 30, 2026

children.label: i
children.text: uranium that has been declared excess to national security needs during or prior to fiscal year 2023;

children.label: I
children.text: directly meets the needs of advanced nuclear reactor developers; but

children.label: II
children.text: has been previously used or fabricated for another purpose;

children.label: iii
children.text: uranium that can meet the needs of advanced nuclear reactor developers after removing radioactive or other contaminants that resulted from previous use or fabrication of the fuel for research, development, demonstration, or deployment activities of the Department, including activities that reduce the environmental liability of the Department by accelerating the processing of uranium from stockpiles designated as waste;

children.label: iv
children.text: uranium from a high-enriched uranium stockpile (excluding stockpiles intended for national security needs), which can be blended with lower assay uranium to become HALEU to meet the needs of advanced nuclear reactor developers; and

children.label: v
children.text: uranium from stockpiles intended for other purposes (excluding stockpiles intended for national security needs), but for which uranium could be swapped or replaced in time in such a manner that would not negatively impact the missions of the Department;

children.label: B
children.text: options for expanding, or establishing new, capabilities or infrastructure to support the processing of uranium from Department inventories;

children.label: C
children.text: options for accelerating the availability of HALEU from HALEU enrichment demonstration projects of the Department;

children.label: D
children.text: options for providing HALEU from domestically enriched HALEU procured by the Department through a competitive process pursuant to the Nuclear Fuel Security Program established under subsection (e)(1);

children.label: E
children.text: options to replenish, as needed, Department stockpiles of uranium made available pursuant to subparagraph (A) with domestically enriched HALEU procured by the Department through a competitive process pursuant to the Nuclear Fuel Security Program established under subsection (e)(1); and

children.label: F
children.text: options that combine 1 or more of the approaches described in subparagraphs (A) through (E) to meet the deadlines described in paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: the final disposition of radioactive waste from uranium that is the subject of a contract for sale, resale, transfer, or lease under this subsection; or

children.label: ii
children.text: environmental cleanup activities.

children.label: i
children.text: may not make commitments under this subsection (including cooperative agreements (used in accordance with ), purchase agreements, guarantees, leases, service contracts, or any other type of commitment) for the purchase or other acquisition of HALEU or LEU unless funds are specifically provided for those purposes in advance in appropriations Acts enacted after ; andsection 6305 of title 31March 9, 2024

children.label: I
children.text: if the full extent of the anticipated costs stemming from the commitment is recorded as an obligation at the time that the commitment is made; and

children.label: II
children.text: to the extent of that up-front obligation recorded in full at that time.

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which the Secretary notifies Congress that the HALEU needs of advanced nuclear reactor developers can be fully met by commercial HALEU suppliers in the United States, as determined by the Secretary, in consultation with U.S. nuclear energy companies; and

children.label: B
children.text: .September 30, 2034

children.label: A
children.text: the activity promotes manufacturing in the United States associated with uranium supply chains; or

children.label: i
children.text: in the United States; or

children.label: I
children.text: the government of that country;

children.label: II
children.text: an associated entity; or

children.label: III
children.text: a U.S. nuclear energy company.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: The Secretary may waive the requirements of paragraph (1) with respect to an activity if the Secretary determines a waiver to be necessary to achieve 1 or more of the objectives described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Reasonable compensation
children.label: j Reasonable compensation
children.text: In carrying out activities under this section, the Secretary shall ensure that any LEU and HALEU made available by the Secretary under 1 or more of the Programs is subject to reasonable compensation, taking into account the fair market value of the LEU or HALEU and the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Nuclear Regulatory Commission
children.label: k Nuclear Regulatory Commission
children.text: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission shall prioritize and expedite consideration of any action related to the Programs to the extent permitted under the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 ( et seq.) and related statutes.42 U.S.C. 2011

children.heading: USEC Privatization Act
children.label: l USEC Privatization Act
children.text: The requirements of section 3112(d)(2) of the USEC Privatization Act () shall not apply to activities related to the Programs.42 U.S.C. 2297h–10(d)(2)

children.heading: National security needs
children.label: m National security needs
children.text: The Secretary shall only make available to a member of the consortium under this section for commercial use or use in a demonstration project material that the President has determined is not necessary for national security needs during or prior to fiscal year 2023, subject to the condition that the material made available shall not include any material that the Secretary determines to be necessary for the National Nuclear Security Administration or any critical mission of the Department.

children.heading: International agreements
children.label: n International agreements
children.text: This section shall be applied in a manner consistent with the obligations of the United States under international agreements.

children.heading: Report on civil nuclear credit program
children.label: o Report on civil nuclear credit program

children.text: the zero-emission nuclear power production credit authorized by ; andsection 45U of title 26
any increased fuel costs associated with the use of domestic fuel that may arise from the implementation of that program.
